user_stories.goal: Generate a personalized day-by-day city itinerary by entering destination, trip length, budget level, interests, and travel style
user_stories.benefit: Save planning time with a clear, ready-to-use itinerary tailored to my preferences
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/itinerary accepts JSON body with keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style and returns HTTP 200 on success
Successful response is JSON with top-level keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, overview, itinerary, and tips
The itinerary array length equals the numeric value of days in the request
Each itinerary day object includes keys: day, morning, afternoon, evening, and budget_note
Activity titles and descriptions are human-readable strings (no raw [object Object] text) and may be inside dictionaries but must render readable fields
Estimated costs are numeric values adjusted according to the budget input (not strings like low/medium/high)

user_stories.goal: Implement a stable Flask API contract that reliably generates itineraries and handles invalid input
user_stories.benefit: Provide a deterministic, testable backend that supports automated tests and Docker deployment
acceptance_criteria: Expose GET /health that returns HTTP 200 and a JSON health object
Expose POST /api/itinerary following the input/output shape specified and return HTTP 400 with a JSON error object for invalid input
Itinerary generation is deterministic for identical inputs (same destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style) to allow automated pytest checks
Activities do not repeat the same generic fallback each day: morning/afternoon/evening selection rotates using destination, day number, time slot, and user interests

Before assembling the itinerary, each user interest is expanded into multiple concrete activities (e.g., food -> breakfast street-food lane, local market tasting, signature restaurant meal, dessert and tea stop, evening snack street)

user_stories.goal: Build a website UI that collects user input, calls the Flask API, and displays a clear day-by-day itinerary
user_stories.benefit: Users can easily plan trips and view readable itineraries without developer intervention
acceptance_criteria: The frontend uses stable element IDs: plannerForm, destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, resultsContainer, daysContainer, formMessage, and errorMessage
The frontend exposes stable functions: collectFormData, validateFormData, setLoading, showError, renderItinerary, renderDay, renderActivity, and formatActivityText
renderItinerary/renderDay/renderActivity produce human-readable text for each activity and never render [object Object]
The frontend must not create its own image preview area (image integration is handled later)
On API error or invalid input the frontend displays a JSON error message in errorMessage and uses HTTP 400 handling

user_stories.goal: Ensure itineraries are diverse, thematic across days, and present realistic per-activity costs
user_stories.benefit: Deliver varied, useful itineraries that feel handcrafted and respect user budget
acceptance_criteria: Each day should, where possible, have a distinct theme or planning focus (e.g., food day, outdoor day, cultural day)
Activities for the same interest are varied across days so a multi-day trip does not repetitively surface the same three activities
Activity allocation rotates across morning, afternoon, and evening so the same activity type is not always in the same timeslot
Budget notes per day explain cost context and are numeric adjustments reflected in per-activity estimated costs
Examples of interest expansions are implemented (food -> multiple concrete food activities; outdoor -> lakefront walk, city park, scenic viewpoint, nature trail)
